title: ISPs' long nightmare of having to list all the fees they charge is finally over
excerpt: FCC lets ISPs stop listing all fees after companies complained it was too hard.
content_html:
In the FCC’s words, these fees “represent costs the provider chooses to pass through to consumers rather than rolling them into the base monthly price.” Ultimately, the fees are part of an ISP’s total cost of doing business and could be reflected in the overall advertised price. But ISPs choose to treat them as separate charges for the purpose of billing consumers.

The burden of listing fees

While ISPs seem to have little difficulty charging the fees when it’s time to issue monthly bills, they complain about the burden of listing all the fees on the broadband price labels. Broadband lobby group USTelecom complained to the FCC that “providers must create and update hundreds of different labels to account for geographic variability and to ensure that their systems properly queue the label specific to the proper location when the customer inputs their address.”

Previous FCC leadership implemented broadband labels in response to a directive from Congress. “Over the last few years, we have learned that when labels become cluttered with unnecessary details, they stop being the quick, easy-to-use shopping tool Congress envisioned,” Carr said at today’s meeting.

In a press release, the FCC said the previous rules resulted in “sometimes-confusing labels that strayed beyond the statutory framework Congress created, increasing compliance costs for providers in the process.” Carr said the new labels will be simpler but that “providers will still offer a label for every standalone broadband plan” with “necessary information about pricing, introductory rates, broadband speeds, and data allowances. The labels will also remain accessible and available in the languages providers use to market their services.”

Another change made today will let ISPs have their customer service reps “discuss the label conversationally over the phone, rather than reading it to consumers verbatim,” the FCC said. Gomez said phone calls remain an important point of sale, particularly for people who don’t currently have broadband service. She said the updated rules “require that critical information is conveyed over the phone, including the monthly price, inclusive of any monthly fees, the introductory rate and its duration, typical download and upload speeds, latency, data allowance, contract term, and early termination fees.”

sentences.en: In the FCC’s words, these fees “represent costs the provider chooses to pass through to consumers rather than rolling them into the base monthly price.”
sentences.zh: 用 FCC 的话说，这些费用“代表提供商选择转嫁给消费者的成本，而不是将其计入每月基本价格”。

sentences.en: Ultimately, the fees are part of an ISP’s total cost of doing business and could be reflected in the overall advertised price.
sentences.zh: 最终，这些费用是 ISP 业务总成本的一部分，并且可以反映在总体广告价格中。

sentences.en: But ISPs choose to treat them as separate charges for the purpose of billing consumers.
sentences.zh: 但互联网服务提供商选择将它们视为单独的费用，以便向消费者计费。

blocks.html_en: While ISPs seem to have little difficulty charging the fees when it’s time to issue monthly bills, they complain about the burden of listing all the fees on the broadband price labels. Broadband lobby group USTelecom complained to the FCC that “providers must create and update hundreds of different labels to account for geographic variability and to ensure that their systems properly queue the label specific to the proper location when the customer inputs their address.”

sentences.en: While ISPs seem to have little difficulty charging the fees when it’s time to issue monthly bills, they complain about the burden of listing all the fees on the broadband price labels.
sentences.zh: 虽然互联网服务提供商在每月发放账单时收取费用似乎没有什么困难，但他们抱怨在宽带价格标签上列出所有费用的负担。

sentences.en: Broadband lobby group USTelecom complained to the FCC that “providers must create and update hundreds of different labels to account for geographic variability and to ensure that their systems properly queue the label specific to the proper location when the customer inputs their address.”
sentences.zh: 宽带游说团体美国电信向 FCC 抱怨称，“提供商必须创建和更新数百个不同的标签，以考虑到地理变化，并确保当客户输入地址时，他们的系统能够正确地将特定于正确位置的标签进行排队。”

blocks.html_en: Previous FCC leadership implemented broadband labels in response to a directive from Congress. “Over the last few years, we have learned that when labels become cluttered with unnecessary details, they stop being the quick, easy-to-use shopping tool Congress envisioned,” Carr said at today’s meeting.

sentences.en: Previous FCC leadership implemented broadband labels in response to a directive from Congress.
sentences.zh: 前任 FCC 领导层根据国会的指示实施了宽带标签。

sentences.en: “Over the last few years, we have learned that when labels become cluttered with unnecessary details, they stop being the quick, easy-to-use shopping tool Congress envisioned,” Carr said at today’s meeting.
sentences.zh: 卡尔在今天的会议上表示：“在过去几年中，我们了解到，当标签上充斥着不必要的细节时，它们就不再是国会所设想的快速、易于使用的购物工具。”

blocks.html_en: In a press release, the FCC said the previous rules resulted in “sometimes-confusing labels that strayed beyond the statutory framework Congress created, increasing compliance costs for providers in the process.” Carr said the new labels will be simpler but that “providers will still offer a label for every standalone broadband plan” with “necessary information about pricing, introductory rates, broadband speeds, and data allowances. The labels will also remain accessible and available in the languages providers use to market their services.”

sentences.en: In a press release, the FCC said the previous rules resulted in “sometimes-confusing labels that strayed beyond the statutory framework Congress created, increasing compliance costs for providers in the process.”
sentences.zh: FCC 在一份新闻稿中表示，之前的规则导致“有时会产生令人困惑的标签，超出了国会创建的法定框架，增加了提供商在此过程中的合规成本。”

sentences.en: Carr said the new labels will be simpler but that “providers will still offer a label for every standalone broadband plan” with “necessary information about pricing, introductory rates, broadband speeds, and data allowances.
sentences.zh: 卡尔表示，新标签将更加简单，但“提供商仍将为每个独立宽带计划提供标签”，其中包含“有关定价、介绍费率、宽带速度和数据限额的必要信息”。

sentences.en: The labels will also remain accessible and available in the languages providers use to market their services.”
sentences.zh: 这些标签也将保持可访问性，并以提供商用于营销其服务的语言提供。”

blocks.html_en: Another change made today will let ISPs have their customer service reps “discuss the label conversationally over the phone, rather than reading it to consumers verbatim,” the FCC said. Gomez said phone calls remain an important point of sale, particularly for people who don’t currently have broadband service. She said the updated rules “require that critical information is conveyed over the phone, including the monthly price, inclusive of any monthly fees, the introductory rate and its duration, typical download and upload speeds, latency, data allowance, contract term, and early termination fees.”

sentences.en: Another change made today will let ISPs have their customer service reps “discuss the label conversationally over the phone, rather than reading it to consumers verbatim,” the FCC said.
sentences.zh: FCC 表示，今天进行的另一项更改将允许 ISP 让其客户服务代表“通过电话以对话方式讨论标签，而不是逐字阅读给消费者”。

sentences.en: Gomez said phone calls remain an important point of sale, particularly for people who don’t currently have broadband service.
sentences.zh: 戈麦斯表示，电话仍然是一个重要的销售点，特别是对于目前没有宽带服务的人来说。

sentences.en: She said the updated rules “require that critical information is conveyed over the phone, including the monthly price, inclusive of any monthly fees, the introductory rate and its duration, typical download and upload speeds, latency, data allowance, contract term, and early termination fees.”
sentences.zh: 她表示，更新后的规则“要求通过电话传达关键信息，包括每月价格（包括任何月费）、介绍费率及其持续时间、典型下载和上传速度、延迟、数据限额、合同期限和提前终止费。”

content_html_zh:
用 FCC 的话说，这些费用“代表提供商选择转嫁给消费者的成本，而不是将其计入每月基本价格”。 最终，这些费用是 ISP 业务总成本的一部分，并且可以反映在总体广告价格中。 但互联网服务提供商选择将它们视为单独的费用，以便向消费者计费。

上市费用的负担

虽然互联网服务提供商在每月发放账单时收取费用似乎没有什么困难，但他们抱怨在宽带价格标签上列出所有费用的负担。 宽带游说团体美国电信向 FCC 抱怨称，“提供商必须创建和更新数百个不同的标签，以考虑到地理变化，并确保当客户输入地址时，他们的系统能够正确地将特定于正确位置的标签进行排队。”

前任 FCC 领导层根据国会的指示实施了宽带标签。 卡尔在今天的会议上表示：“在过去几年中，我们了解到，当标签上充斥着不必要的细节时，它们就不再是国会所设想的快速、易于使用的购物工具。”

FCC 在一份新闻稿中表示，之前的规则导致“有时会产生令人困惑的标签，超出了国会创建的法定框架，增加了提供商在此过程中的合规成本。” 卡尔表示，新标签将更加简单，但“提供商仍将为每个独立宽带计划提供标签”，其中包含“有关定价、介绍费率、宽带速度和数据限额的必要信息”。 这些标签也将保持可访问性，并以提供商用于营销其服务的语言提供。”

FCC 表示，今天进行的另一项更改将允许 ISP 让其客户服务代表“通过电话以对话方式讨论标签，而不是逐字阅读给消费者”。 戈麦斯表示，电话仍然是一个重要的销售点，特别是对于目前没有宽带服务的人来说。 她表示，更新后的规则“要求通过电话传达关键信息，包括每月价格（包括任何月费）、介绍费率及其持续时间、典型下载和上传速度、延迟、数据限额、合同期限和提前终止费。”
